event: The Fall — Eve and the Serpent
symbols: serpent coiling around Eve — the tempter deceiving the first woman
tree with fruit — the tree of knowledge of good and evil
Adam in background — the passive partner who also ate
swan in water — beauty and innocence of creation before the Fall
garden setting — Eden, the paradise about to be lost
description: A pen-and-ink drawing with a surrealist quality. In the foreground, a nude woman (Eve) stands with a large serpent coiling around her body. Behind and above on a hillside, a nude male figure (Adam) sits beneath a tree with round fruits. In the lower right, a swan glides on water. The style is loose and illustrative with fine line work. The initials 'G.R.D.' appear at lower right. The scene is set in a garden landscape with grass, water, and trees.
theological_description: This illustration depicts the Fall of humanity in the Garden of Eden as narrated in Genesis 3. The serpent, 'more crafty than any other beast of the field that the Lord God had made' (Gen 3:1), tempts Eve by questioning God's command. Eve is shown already entangled with the serpent, representing her yielding to temptation: 'she took of its fruit and ate, and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate' (Gen 3:6). Adam sits passively in the background by the tree, echoing Paul's observation that 'Adam was not deceived, but the woman was deceived and became a transgressor' (1 Tim 2:14). The swan and peaceful water represent the pristine creation about to be corrupted by sin. The Genesis narrative teaches that through this act of disobedience, 'sin came into the world through one man, and death through sin' (Rom 5:12). The scene captures the moment before paradise is irrevocably lost.

scripture_refs.reason: The serpent tempts Eve, she eats and gives to Adam — the Fall narrative
The serpent deceived me and I ate — Eve's entanglement with the serpent
Sin came into the world through one man, and death through sin
Adam was not deceived but the woman was — Adam passive in the background
